task_description:
# PocketBase view collection: user_post_stats

## Goal
Configure the PocketBase v0.31.0 application at `/home/user/myproject` so that it exposes a read-only **view collection** named `user_post_stats`. The view must aggregate, for every user in the existing `users` (auth) collection, the total number of `posts` that reference the user as their `author` and the timestamp of that user's most recent post.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Project path: `/home/user/myproject`
- Start command: `go run . serve --http=127.0.0.1:8090`
- Port: `8090`
- A view collection named `user_post_stats` must be present in the running PocketBase instance.
- API endpoint `GET /api/collections/user_post_stats/records?sort=-post_count` must respond with HTTP `200` when called by an authenticated superuser. The JSON response must contain an `items` array.
- Every entry of `items` must include all of the following fields:
    - `id` — a non-empty string that matches the `id` of a record in the built-in `users` collection (one row per user).
    - `user` — the same user id.
    - `email` — the user's email address.
    - `post_count` — an integer `≥ 0` equal to the actual number of `posts` whose `author` field references that user.
    - `last_post_at` — the most recent `posts.created` timestamp (PocketBase RFC3339-style string, e.g. `"2024-11-10 18:45:27.123Z"`) for that user, or an empty string `""` when the user has no posts.
- The endpoint must support PocketBase filtering. In particular, `GET /api/collections/user_post_stats/records?filter=(post_count%3E0)` must return HTTP `200` and only contain users whose `post_count` is strictly greater than zero.
- Sorting by `-post_count` must order results by descending post count.
task_integrations: - None (PocketBase runs locally inside the container; no external services are involved.)
truth:
# Verification Plan

## Setup
1. If a PocketBase process is currently listening on port `8090`, terminate it so the verifier can start a fresh server.
2. `cd /home/user/myproject`.
3. Start the server with the command from the Acceptance Criteria section: `go run . serve --http=127.0.0.1:8090 &`. Wait until TCP port `8090` accepts connections (timeout ~180s while Go rebuilds).

## Verification Steps
1. Read superuser credentials from the `POCKETBASE_SUPERUSER_EMAIL` and `POCKETBASE_SUPERUSER_PASSWORD` environment variables. Authenticate via `POST /api/collections/_superusers/auth-with-password` with body `{"identity": <email>, "password": <password>}`. Save the returned `token` for use in subsequent requests as the `Authorization` header value.
2. Seed three users via `POST /api/collections/users/records` using the superuser token. Use unique emails that include a random suffix (e.g. `f"alice-{uuid4().hex[:8]}@example.com"`) so the test is rerunnable. Use password `"Pass1234!"` and `passwordConfirm` `"Pass1234!"`. Capture each user's returned `id` as `alice_id`, `bob_id`, `carol_id`. Save the emails actually used as `alice_email`, `bob_email`, `carol_email`.
3. Seed posts via `POST /api/collections/posts/records` using the superuser token:
   - For `alice_id`: create 3 records with titles `"A1"`, `"A2"`, `"A3"`. Insert a brief delay (e.g. `time.sleep(0.05)`) between requests so the auto-generated `created` timestamps are monotonically increasing. Save the `created` value of the last post (`"A3"`) as `alice_latest_created`.
   - For `bob_id`: create 1 record with title `"B1"`. Save its `created` value as `bob_latest_created`.
   - `carol_id`: create no posts.
4. Send `GET /api/collections/user_post_stats/records?sort=-post_count&perPage=200` with the superuser token. Assert:
   - HTTP status `200`.
   - Response JSON contains an `items` array.
   - There exists a row in `items` whose `id == alice_id`. For that row: `user == alice_id`, `email == alice_email`, `post_count == 3`, `last_post_at` is a non-empty string equal to `alice_latest_created`.
   - There exists a row whose `id == bob_id`. For that row: `user == bob_id`, `email == bob_email`, `post_count == 1`, `last_post_at` equals `bob_latest_created`.
   - There exists a row whose `id == carol_id`. For that row: `user == carol_id`, `email == carol_email`, `post_count == 0`, `last_post_at == ""`.
   - Considering only the rows whose `id` is one of `{alice_id, bob_id, carol_id}`, when scanned in the response order the relative order is `alice_id` (post_count 3) before `bob_id` (post_count 1) before `carol_id` (post_count 0). This confirms `sort=-post_count` is respected.
5. Send `GET /api/collections/user_post_stats/records?filter=(post_count%3E0)&perPage=200` with the superuser token. Assert:
   - HTTP status `200`.
   - The set of `id` values from the returned `items` contains `alice_id` and `bob_id`.
   - The set of `id` values does NOT contain `carol_id`.
   - Every returned item has `post_count > 0`.
